metadata.purpose: Assess prerequisite knowledge before learning 4-digit place value

questions.question: What is the place value of 5 in the number 253?
questions.explanation: The digit 5 is in the tens place. To find place value, multiply the digit by its place: 5 × 10 = 50.

commonMistakes.mistake: Confused face value (5) with place value (50)
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding difference between face value and place value
commonMistakes.remediation: Watch video on Face Value vs Place Value

commonMistakes.mistake: Gave place name instead of numerical value
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Place value is a number, not a position name
commonMistakes.remediation: Review how to calculate place value

questions.question: What is the place value of 8 in the number 687?
questions.explanation: The digit 8 is in the tens place, so its place value is 8 × 10 = 80.

commonMistakes.mistake: Selected face value instead of place value

questions.question: Which number is greater: 456 or 465?
questions.explanation: Compare digit by digit from left. Hundreds: both have 4. Tens: 6 vs 5. Since 6 > 5, we know 465 > 456.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Always compare from left to right (highest place first)

questions.question: What is 7 × 100?
questions.explanation: When you multiply by 100, add two zeros to the number. 7 × 100 = 700.

commonMistakes.mistake: Multiplied by 10 instead of 100 (added one zero instead of two)
Added three zeros instead of two

questions.question: In the number 834, which digit is in the hundreds place?
questions.explanation: In a 3-digit number, the first digit (from left) is in the hundreds place. So in 834, the digit 8 is in the hundreds place.

commonMistakes.mistake: Counted from right instead of left
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Place positions are read from left to right

questions.question: What is 10 × 10?
questions.explanation: 10 × 10 = 100. When you multiply 10 by itself, you get 100.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with > or <: 789 ___ 798
questions.explanation: Compare hundreds: 7 = 7. Compare tens: 8 < 9. Since 8 is less than 9 in the tens place, 789 < 798.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Remember: smaller number goes on the smaller side of the symbol

questions.question: What number comes after 999?
questions.explanation: After 999 (nine hundred ninety-nine) comes 1000 (one thousand). This is the first 4-digit number.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thought you add another 9 instead of rolling over to 1000
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding how place value rolls over when counting

questions.question: True or False: In the number 642, the digit 6 represents 600.
questions.explanation: True! The digit 6 is in the hundreds place, so it represents 6 × 100 = 600.

questions.question: If you count by hundreds from 200, which number will you say: 200, 300, 400, ?
questions.explanation: When counting by hundreds, you add 100 each time. 200 + 100 = 300, 300 + 100 = 400, 400 + 100 = 500.

excellent.message: Excellent! You have strong prerequisite knowledge. You're ready to learn about 4-digit numbers.

good.message: Good work! You have the necessary prerequisite knowledge to proceed.

partial.message: You have some gaps in prerequisite knowledge. Watch review videos before learning new content.

needs_review.message: You need to review prerequisite concepts before learning 4-digit numbers.

feedbackMessages.on_completion: Great job completing the readiness assessment!
feedbackMessages.low_score_encouragement: Don't worry! We'll show you videos to help you review the concepts you need.
feedbackMessages.high_score_praise: Fantastic! You're well-prepared to learn about 4-digit numbers.
feedbackMessages.retake_option: After watching the recommended videos, you can retake this assessment to check your progress.
